yesterdayAnalysis: Monday was a scheduled full rest day post-SDIT (San Diego International Triathlon on 6/28), which was executed perfectly with no training stress logged. This strategic recovery day was essential after completing the 91 TSS race simulation that included swim, bike, and run disciplines. The complete rest aligns with the sharpener phase protocol of minimizing fatigue before Oregon 70.3.
todayRecommendation: Execute the planned 30-minute easy recovery spin only — expect legs to feel heavy and resist any temptation to add intensity. This is active recovery to promote blood flow while preserving freshness for Thursday's critical 3x8min race-pace bike intervals.
weekProgress: Week 11 of 13 is on track with 0 of 340 TSS target through two days. This is exactly as planned — the week is heavily back-loaded with Thursday's race-pace bike session, Saturday's surge run, and supporting swim work still ahead. Monday rest and Tuesday recovery spin were both scheduled low-stress sessions.
keyInsight: With 19 days to Oregon 70.3, this is the final race-pace stimulus week before taper — Thursday's 3x8min at 145-152w NP is the priority session that must be executed fresh.
